title: Guzmania palustris
intro: Guzmania palustris, also known as the swamp guzmania or bog guzmania, is a species of epiphytic plant in the family Bromeliaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Guzmania palustris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Guzmania palustris is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
